message:
fix(choreography): preserve branch structure in single-sided choice projection + unique channel scaffolding

Two fixes in the obligation choreography subsystem:

1. project_choice (mod.rs): When only one branch of a global choice
   contained local actions for a participant, the projection collapsed
   to just that branch, losing the control-flow semantics. The
   participant would unconditionally execute actions that should be
   conditional on the choice predicate. Now preserves the
   InternalChoice/ExternalChoice wrapper with the empty branch set to
   LocalType::End, maintaining correct control flow regardless of
   which branch has local work.

2. render_lab_test (pipeline.rs): Channel scaffold generation now
   produces directional names (chan_A_to_B, chan_B_to_A) instead of
   ambiguous pair names (chan_A, chan_B). Also adds a composition step
   where each participant's entry channel is assembled from its
   directional endpoints, giving the test skeleton enough structure to
   wire up multi-party session types without name collisions. Test
   verifies uniqueness of per-participant channel entry placeholders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
